kernel_funcs

Classes

KernelFactory

Factory for generating different kernel functions (SE, RQ, SineExp, Matérn)

Module Contents

class kernel_funcs.KernelFactory(dim, normalize, differences_by_dim, n_order, true_noise_std=None)[source]

Factory for generating different kernel functions (SE, RQ, SineExp, Matérn) in isotropic and anisotropic forms.

dim[source]

Dimensionality of the input space.

Type:

int

normalize[source]

Whether to normalize inputs (scaling differences to [-3, 3]).

Type:

bool

differences_by_dim[source]

Pairwise differences between input points, by dimension.

Type:

list of arrays

true_noise_std[source]

Known noise standard deviation (for adjusting noise bounds).

Type:

float, optional

bounds[source]

Hyperparameter bounds (log10 space).

Type:

list of tuples

nu[source]

Smoothness parameter for the Matérn kernel.

Type:

float

n_order[source]

Order of derivatives for kernel smoothness.

Type:

int

dim[source]
normalize[source]
differences_by_dim[source]
true_noise_std = None[source]
bounds = [][source]
nu[source]
n_order[source]
get_bounds_from_data()[source]

Computes bounds for hyperparameters based on the observed data range.

create_kernel(kernel_name, kernel_type)[source]

Returns a kernel function based on specified name and type.

Parameters:

  • kernel_name (str) – Name of the kernel (‘SE’, ‘RQ’, ‘SineExp’, ‘Matern’).

  • kernel_type (str) – Type of kernel (‘anisotropic’ or ‘isotropic’).

Returns:

The selected kernel function.

Return type:

callable

_create_anisotropic(kernel)[source]

Sets bounds and returns the anisotropic kernel function.

Returns:

The anisotropic kernel function.

Return type:

callable

_create_isotropic(kernel)[source]

Sets bounds and returns the isotropic kernel function.

Returns:

The isotropic kernel function.

Return type:

callable

_add_bounds(extra_bounds)[source]

Append additional hyperparameter bounds to the kernel’s configuration.

Parameters:

extra_bounds (list of tuple) – Bounds to append, where each tuple is a (min, max) pair in log10 scale.

se_kernel_anisotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Anisotropic Squared Exponential (SE) kernel.

Parameters:

  • differences_by_dim (list of ndarray)

  • length_scales (list)

  • index (int, optional)

Return type:

ndarray

rq_kernel_anisotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Anisotropic Rational Quadratic (RQ) kernel using in-place operations.

Parameters:

  • differences_by_dim (list of ndarray)

  • length_scales (list)

  • index (int, optional)

Return type:

ndarray

sine_exp_kernel_anisotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Anisotropic Sine-Exponential kernel using in-place operations.

This is also known as the Exp-Sine-Squared or Periodic kernel.

Parameters:

  • differences_by_dim (list of ndarray)

  • length_scales (list)

  • index (int, optional)

Return type:

ndarray

matern_kernel_anisotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Anisotropic Matérn kernel (half-integer ν).

se_kernel_isotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Isotropic Squared Exponential (SE) kernel.

rq_kernel_isotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Isotropic Rational Quadratic (RQ) kernel.

sine_exp_kernel_isotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Isotropic Sine-Exponential kernel.

matern_kernel_isotropic(differences_by_dim, length_scales, index=-1)[source]

Isotropic Matérn kernel (half-integer ν).
